title: Ziploc Bag Organizer
summary: A slotted plywood-topped box that holds bag boxes upright and lets you pull one bag at a time through cutouts in the lid.
description:
Storing bag boxes upright in a divided tray beats the usual junk-drawer approach — this box holds a few sizes side by side, standing on end, so you can see and grab what you need without digging.

The cutouts in the top are the part to slow down for: mark them out, clamp the panel down firmly, drill a starter hole in each corner, and cut just inside your lines with a jigsaw fitted with a fine finish blade for wood. Sand the cut edges smooth afterward — this detail matters more here than on most projects, because a rough edge will snag and tear the bags every time you pull one through.

tools.note: Fitted with a fine finish blade for the top cutouts.
Securing the top panel while cutting the openings.

cutList.note: Mark and cut openings for pulling bags through.

steps.title: Build the divider section
steps.body: Attach the two divider end pieces to two of the 12-1/2" long 1x3s, set in 3-1/2" from each end, using 1-1/4" brad nails and wood glue.

steps.title: Attach the sides and top frame
steps.body: Attach the remaining long side and the top/side frame pieces to the divider section with 1-1/4" brad nails and wood glue.

steps.title: Attach the bottom
steps.body: Attach the plywood bottom to the underside of the frame with glue and 5/8" brad nails.

steps.title: Cut and attach the top
steps.body: Mark out the pull-through cutouts on the remaining plywood panel. Clamp it firmly to a worktable and drill a starter hole in each cutout. Cut just inside your lines with a jigsaw and a fine finish blade — a scroll saw or router with the right bit works too. Sand the cut edges smooth so they won't snag or tear the bags. Attach the finished top to the frame with glue and 5/8" brad nails.

images.alt: A small divided box organizer for storing bag boxes with pull-through cutouts on top
